name: Procedural Level Rooms
description: A room-selection and placement generator that instantiates authored or parameterized room templates into a validated layout with sockets, collision checks, and fallback rules.
design_notes: Procedural level rooms own the concrete content placement layer: choose room_pool_id entries, align doorway sockets, reserve critical-path and optional-branch rooms, run overlap and navigation checks, then commit a room layout that can be saved. The topology should come from a room connection graph; this entry is about selecting and placing actual room content against that topology.
design_purpose: Create replayable dungeon, mission, or building layouts from reusable room content.
Let designers author high-quality room pieces while the generator controls order, branches, and pacing.
Guarantee that starts, exits, rewards, hazards, and objectives fit the generated route.
player_fantasy: The player feels each run or mission has a new layout while rooms still look intentional and playable.

parameters.name: room_pool_id
parameters.description: Content pool used to select eligible authored room templates for the current layout role.

parameters.name: room_template_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for an authored or generated room template.

parameters.name: doorway_socket_id
parameters.description: Connection point used to align rooms, corridors, or vertical links.

parameters.name: placement_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule describing where the room may appear in the graph or layout.

parameters.name: fallback_template_id
parameters.description: Template used when constraints fail after validation_retries.

parameters.name: validation_retries
parameters.description: Attempts to place or repair a room before falling back.

parameters.name: room_layout_stream_key
parameters.description: RNG stream used for room choice and placement so content changes do not disturb unrelated rolls.

parameters.name: room_content_version
parameters.description: Version of the room pool used to reload old layouts and reproduce bad seeds.

edge_cases: A required doorway socket is blocked by props, hazards, or collision after decoration.
The room pool runs out of templates matching the needed size, tag, or critical path role.
A lock room appears before the key room or ability unlock that opens it.
Spawn points inside a valid room fail because they are too close to the player or outside navmesh.
Socket rotation or mirroring makes rooms appear connected while one-way doors, stairs, or vertical links face the wrong direction.
A content patch removes or renames a room_template_id used by an in-progress save.
Fallback room retries use the same invalid pool and never escape the failed placement constraint.
common_bugs: Rooms align visually but their navigation meshes, collision, or door triggers do not connect.
Template IDs are selected by array index, so adding a room changes old seeds unpredictably.
Validation checks only room graph connectivity and misses actual unreachable areas after decoration.
Fallback rooms lack required tags and break quest, loot, or boss placement later in the pipeline.
Only the seed is saved, so a content-version change regenerates a different room layout after reload.
Door trigger ownership is copied from the previous prefab instance and opens the wrong connected room.
balancing_notes: Room pool variety should be tuned against session length; too few templates expose repetition quickly.
Critical path rooms should teach mechanics and scale risk before optional branches add complexity.
Fallback templates should be safe but not so rewarding that failed generation becomes optimal.
accessibility_notes: Ensure generated room readability with landmarks, exits, and map support rather than relying on memorized layouts.
Avoid narrow procedural routes that require precision movement unless the game clearly supports it.

implementation_notes.unity: Instantiate room prefabs by room_pool_id, room_template_id, and doorway_socket_id, then run validation for door triggers, collision, navmesh links, spawn points, and objective reachability before committing the layout.
implementation_notes.godot: Use PackedScene room templates with exported socket markers; build a logical room graph first, then instance scenes, persist selected room_template_id values, and run navigation/connectivity checks.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use level instances, DataAssets, or PCG components for room templates; validate sockets, navmesh, gameplay tags, lock/key ordering, and content version before making the level playable.
implementation_notes.web: Represent rooms and sockets as data, use deterministic template selection by room_layout_stream_key, and export graph plus placement failures for reproducible seed debugging.

relationships.reason: Room placement needs a connection graph to define route structure, branch roles, and reachability before room templates commit.
Room selection and socket placement need stable RNG streams, content versioning, and seed logs for reproducible layouts.
Lock-and-key puzzles can soft-lock procedural rooms unless dependency order and fallback rooms are validated.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses authored chunks assembled into generated levels.

example_games.title: Enter the Gungeon
example_games.note: Uses room-based dungeon layouts with combat and reward rooms.
